title: peplis australis var. biflora
intro: Peplis australis var. biflora is a type of flowering plant, also known as the forked pepli, belonging to the family Menyanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. It has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Traditional herbal preparations of Peplis australis var. biflora include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
